title: Quiz: Quantum Advantage
description: Test your understanding of quantum computational advantage foundations

questions.question: What is the complexity class BQP?

options.text: Problems solvable by quantum computers in polynomial space
Decision problems solvable by quantum computers in polynomial time with bounded error
Problems that can be verified by quantum computers in polynomial time
Problems solvable by quantum computers with exponential speedup

questions.explanation: BQP (Bounded-Error Quantum Polynomial Time) is the class of decision problems solvable by a quantum computer in polynomial time with error probability at most 1/3.

questions.question: Which is TRUE about the relationship between BQP and NP?

options.text: BQP is proven to contain NP
NP is proven to contain BQP
BQP and NP are proven to be equal
The relationship between BQP and NP is unknown

questions.explanation: The relationship between BQP and NP remains one of the major open questions in computational complexity.

questions.question: What type of speedup does Grover's search algorithm provide?

options.text: Exponential speedup
Super-polynomial speedup
Quadratic (polynomial) speedup
Constant speedup

questions.explanation: Grover's algorithm provides a quadratic speedup: O(sqrt(N)) quantum vs O(N) classical.

questions.question: What does Cross-Entropy Benchmark (XEB) measure?

options.text: The entropy of the quantum state
How well sampled bitstrings match the ideal probability distribution
The gate error rate of the quantum device
The classical simulation time required

questions.explanation: XEB measures correlation between sampled bitstrings and ideal probability distribution.

questions.question: For XEB formula F_XEB = 2^n * <p(x)> - 1, what is expected for uniform random sampling?
questions.explanation: For uniform random sampling, <p(x)> = 1/2^n, so XEB = 2^n * (1/2^n) - 1 = 0.

questions.question: What is a key requirement for valid quantum supremacy demonstration?

options.text: The task must have immediate practical applications
The quantum computer must use fault-tolerant qubits
The output must be verifiable (at least statistically)
The speedup must be at least 10,000x

questions.explanation: A valid supremacy demonstration requires verifiable output to confirm correctness.

questions.question: Which are TRUE about Shor's algorithm? (Select all that apply)

options.text: It provides exponential speedup for integer factoring
Its speedup relies on an oracle
It breaks RSA cryptography
It proves P = NP

questions.explanation: Shor's provides exponential speedup for factoring and breaks RSA. It does NOT rely on an oracle and does NOT prove P=NP.

questions.question: Classical simulation methods can never be improved beyond current capabilities.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: False. Classical simulation methods continue to improve, requiring rigorous asymptotic analysis.
